description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly reddish-brown coloration. The surface displays natural striations and banding, with lighter and darker tones of brown and tan running in wavy, layered patterns, suggesting sedimentary origins. The rock's texture appears coarse and uneven, with jagged edges and a few smoother, weathered patches. Small pits and indentations are visible, adding to its naturalistic appearance. The overall mass and density suggest it is composed primarily of stone, likely sandstone or a similar sedimentary rock. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, reddish-brown rock with layered striations and a coarse, angular surface. Its natural, weathered appearance features jagged edges and varied brown tones.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's angular peak and prominent striations, with lighter brown bands and a rough, jagged edge.
description_view_1: The rock appears darker and more rounded from this angle, with less visible banding and a broader, flatter surface.
description_view_2: This side shows a wide, relatively flat face with pronounced horizontal striations and a mix of dark and light brown patches.
description_view_3: A balanced view of the rock's sloped surface, displaying the layered structure and a mix of sharp and smooth contours.
